title: example_cc in the documentation failing to compile
body:
The example given in the https://github.com/verilator/verilator/blob/master/docs/guide/example_cc.rst fails to compile with the given command `verilator -Wall --cc --exe --build sim_main.cpp our.v`, with the following compiler error:
```
../sim_main.cpp:4:7: error: ‘VerilatedContext’ was not declared in this scope; did you mean ‘VerilatedMutex’?
```
This is with `Verilator 4.106 2020-12-02 rev v4.106`, which I am just starting to play around with and later want to use with cocotb.
Anyway, since the examples given in https://github.com/verilator/verilator/tree/master/examples work just fine, I could just rewrite this documentation page `make_hello_c` example.
Is that alright for me to do? If so, since I did not contributed to the project in the past, do I just edit the page in question and submit a pull request?

Cheers.
code_blocks: ../sim_main.cpp:4:7: error: ‘VerilatedContext’ was not declared in this scope; did you mean ‘VerilatedMutex’?
